title: alyssum minus subsp. strigosum
intro: Alyssum minus subsp. strigosum, also known as lesser alpine alyssum, is a low-growing, perennial plant that belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. The plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, offering various health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The leaves and flowers of alyssum minus subsp. strigosum can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
